explanations.3: This starts with a pre-built image that has the .NET SDK (the compiler and build tools), and tags this stage as 'build' so we can reference it later.
explanations.13: This starts a new, smaller image with just the .NET runtime (no compiler), and tags it 'final' because this will be our actual application container.
explanations.17: This copies only the compiled output from the 'build' stage into the 'final' stage, so the final image doesn't include the compiler or source code—making it much smaller.
explanations.20: This declares a build argument (a variable you can pass in) that specifies which version of Oracle's client library to download.
explanations.29: This long chain of commands downloads the Oracle client, extracts it, sets up symbolic links (shortcuts to specific library versions), and registers those libraries so the system can find them.
explanations.33: The `ln -s` command creates a symbolic link: a shortcut from `libclntsh.so` to the specific version number, so the application can find the library without knowing the exact version.
